targets.title: Extreme Value Theory x Proteome Thermal Vulnerability Mapping
targets.field_a: Extreme value statistics (Gumbel/Frechet/Weibull distributions, return level estimation, tail index analysis)
targets.field_c: Proteome-wide thermal stability distributions and organism thermal limits
bridge_concepts: Generalized extreme value (GEV) distribution fitting to proteome Tm data
Return level estimation for organism thermal death point
Tail index (xi parameter) classification — Weibull vs Frechet domain
Peaks-over-threshold (POT) method with generalized Pareto distribution for vulnerable protein subset
Fisher-Tippett-Gnedenko theorem guaranteeing GEV convergence
targets.web_verification_note: Zero papers found applying EVT/Gumbel/Weibull to proteome thermal stability

targets.title: Reservoir Computing Theory x Gut Microbiome Community Dynamics
targets.field_a: Reservoir computing theory (echo state networks, liquid state machines, edge-of-chaos dynamics)
targets.field_c: Gut microbiome temporal dynamics, resilience, and dysbiosis
bridge_concepts: Echo state property (ESP) verification on microbiome interaction matrix
Memory capacity MC from longitudinal microbiome + diet time series
Maximum Lyapunov exponent lambda as health/dysbiosis indicator
Spectral radius rho(W) of species interaction matrix
Input separability via Shannon mutual information I(community_state; diet_history)
targets.web_verification_note: 2024 bioRxiv has single-strain bacterial RC but NO community-as-reservoir-computer framing

targets.title: Granular Jamming Transition Physics x Chromatin Compaction During Confined Migration
targets.field_a: Granular jamming physics (jamming transition, random close packing, force chains, marginally jammed states)
targets.field_c: Chromatin compaction mechanics during cancer cell migration through constrictions
bridge_concepts: Jamming transition volume fraction phi_J for nucleosome packings
Coordination number Z_c = 2d (isostatic condition) for nucleosome contacts
Force chains in jammed chromatin — anisotropic stress transmission
Power-law scaling G' ~ (phi - phi_J)^alpha near jamming
Gardner transition — hierarchical metastable states explaining irreversible chromatin rearrangements
targets.web_verification_note: Zero results for chromatin jamming or nucleosome jamming. Chromatin-as-glass exists but jamming framework absent.

targets.title: ML-Augmented Acoustic Emission Classification x Plant Xylem Cavitation Mode Identification
targets.field_a: ML-enhanced acoustic emission analysis (CWT scalograms + CNN) for failure mode classification in composites
targets.field_c: Plant xylem cavitation and embolism detection during drought stress
bridge_concepts: Continuous wavelet transform (CWT) scalogram generation from raw AE waveforms
CNN architecture transfer (InceptionTime/ResNet) via domain adaptation
Wavelet packet decomposition (WPD) energy distribution as failure mode fingerprint
Peak frequency x duration x rise time feature space (ASTM E1930)
Felicity ratio (Kaiser effect) mapping to cavitation reversibility
targets.web_verification_note: Basic AE for plant cavitation exists (frequency thresholding). Advanced CWT+CNN pipeline from NDT NOT transferred.

targets.title: Classical Nucleation Theory x Ferroptosis Ferritin Iron Pool Dynamics
targets.field_a: Classical nucleation theory (CNT) — nucleation kinetics, critical radius, supersaturation-dependent crystallization
targets.field_c: Intracellular iron pool dynamics during ferroptosis — ferritinophagy, LIP, ferrihydrite dissolution
bridge_concepts: CNT nucleation rate equation J = A*exp(-DeltaG*/kT)
Critical nucleus radius r* = 2*gamma/|DeltaG_v| — pH-dependent iron fate
Ostwald ripening kinetics (LSW theory) for ferrihydrite nanoparticle coarsening
Surface energy gamma of ferrihydrite (~0.5-0.8 J/m2, Navrotsky group)
Mineral dissolution rate law: rate = k*(1-S)^n for chelator-dependent iron release
targets.web_verification_note: 2025 JACS ferritin nucleation cryo-EM exists but in structural biology context. Zero papers apply CNT to ferroptosis iron dynamics. Key anomaly confirmed: LIP does NOT expand during ferroptosis (2025 bioRxiv).
targets.key_anomaly: LIP does NOT expand during ferroptosis despite ferritinophagy — CNT explains via nanoparticulate iron invisible to LIP probes
